task_completion.description: The gapminder data is loaded and the report page is renamed to 'Global Health'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data pane on the right shows the loaded table with columns like 'country', 'gdpPercap', and 'lifeExp'. The page tab at the bottom of the canvas is named 'Global Health'.

task_completion.description: A calculated column named 'Income_Group' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data pane, there is a field named 'Income_Group' with a calculated column icon (typically a table icon with a vertical highlight or 'fx' symbol).

task_completion.description: A DAX measure named 'Avg_Life_Expectancy' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Data pane, there is a field named 'Avg_Life_Expectancy' with a calculator icon next to it.

task_completion.description: A Filled Map visual is created showing country as location and lifeExp as color saturation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A map visual is present on the canvas with countries shaded in varying colors. If selected, the Visualizations pane shows 'country' in the Location well and 'lifeExp' in the Color saturation/Tooltips well.

task_completion.description: A Scatter Chart visual is created with gdpPercap (X-axis), lifeExp (Y-axis), and pop (Size).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A scatter plot is present on the canvas with bubbles of varying sizes. The X-axis should represent GDP values (ranging up to ~113k) and the Y-axis should represent life expectancy (ranging from ~20 to ~90).

task_completion.description: A Table visual is created displaying country, continent, lifeExp, gdpPercap, and pop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A standard table grid is visible on the canvas containing columns for country, continent, lifeExp, gdpPercap, and pop.

task_completion.description: The report is saved as Global_Health_Explorer.pbix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar at the top of the Power BI Desktop window reads 'Global_Health_Explorer' or 'Global_Health_Explorer - Power BI Desktop'.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Power BI Desktop application to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the authentic Microsoft Power BI Desktop interface, including the ribbon, canvas, Visualizations pane, and Data pane, rather than a mock-up or alternative software.

integrity.description: The agent did not use Python or terminal scripts to generate static images of charts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visuals are interactive Power BI components within the Power BI canvas. There are no Python IDEs, Jupyter notebooks, or terminal windows visible generating the plots.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual provided dataset rather than manually entering a few dummy rows.
integrity.visual_evidence: The scatter chart and table show a dense dataset with many data points (representing the 142 unique countries / 1704 rows), not just a handful of manually typed entries.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Gapminder dataset contains 1704 rows and 142 unique countries. GDP per capita (gdpPercap) ranges from 241.17 to 113,523.13, and life expectancy (lifeExp) ranges from 23.60 to 82.60. The Scatter Chart X-axis should reflect GDP values up to roughly 100k-120k, and the Y-axis should reflect life expectancy between 20 and 90. The 'Income_Group' logic categorizes the data into High (164 rows), Medium (528 rows), and Low (1012 rows). The required model items are a calculated column 'Income_Group' (column icon) and a DAX measure 'Avg_Life_Expectancy' (calculator icon).
